body:
H I G H C O U R T O F A U S T R A L I A
NOTICE OF FILING This document was filed electronically in the High Court of Australia on 08 Jul 2025 and has been accepted for filing under the High Court Rules 2004. Details of filing and important additional information are provided below. Details of Filing File Number: File Title: S94/2025 AA v. The Trustees of the Roman Catholic Church for the Diocese of Maitland-Newcastle ABN 79469343054 Registry: Sydney Document filed: Filing party: Date filed: Form 27B - Appellant's chronology Appellant 08 Jul 2025 Important Information This Notice has been inserted as the cover page of the document which has been accepted for filing electronically. It is now taken to be part of that document for the purposes of the proceeding in the Court and contains important information for all parties to that proceeding. It must be included in the document served on each of those parties and whenever the document is reproduced for use by the Court. Appellant S94/2025

-2-
Date Event Reference 15 January 1969 Father Pickin appointed to Wallsend parish CAB 11; SC[10.5] 1969 1969 1969 1969 1969 1969 Mid 1969 Father Pickin attended Wallsend High School to provide religious education classes to students CAB 11; SC[10.5] The appellant, aged 13, is in year 8 (second form) at Wallsend High School, and is taught religious education by Fr Pickin CAB 11; SC[10.6], [10.7] A friend of the appellant, Mr Alan Perry, is also taught religious education by Fr Pickin CAB 38; SC[137] Fr Pickin invites both the appellant and Mr Perry to the Presbytery of St Patrick’s Church on multiple occasions CAB 11; SC[10.7] The appellant and Mr Perry attend the Presbytery of St Patrick’s Church with Fr Pickin on Friday nights on multiple occasions CAB 11; SC[10.7], [10.8] While at the Presbytery, Fr Pickin gave both the appellant and Mr Perry beer and cigarettes CAB 11; SC[10.9] The appellant was sexually abused on multiple occasions by Fr Ronald Pickin in the bedroom of the presbytery of St Patrick’s Catholic Church, Wallsend CAB 42; SC[154] CAB 48; SC[182] CAB 56; SC[213] Late 1970s BB sexually abused by Father Pickin CAB 28; SC[84] CAB 154; CA[100] 15 March 2024 Appellant commences proceedings in the Supreme Court of New South Wales 4 June 2024 Appellant files Amended Statement of Claim ABFM 10 12 June 2024 Appellant gives evidence on commission CAB 10; SC[7] 23-29 August 2024 Trial heard before Primary Judge (Schmidt AJ) 20 September 2024 Primary Judge (Schmidt AJ) delivers judgment on liability 18 October 2024 Primary Judge (Schmidt AJ) enters judgment for the applicant in the amount of $636,480.00 CAB 5 CAB 5 CAB 91

-3-
Date Event 4 November 2024 Respondent files a Notice of Appeal against the decision of the Primary Judge Reference CAB 105 26 November 2024 Appellant files Notice of Contention in the CAB 111 New South Wales Court of Appeal 27 February 2025 Hearing in the New South Wales Court of Appeal (Bell CJ, Leeming JA and Ball JA) CAB 114 15 April 2025 The New South Wales Court of Appeal (Bell CJ, Leeming JA and Ball JA) deliver reasons and orders, in respect of proceedings 2024/00406824 allowing the appeal CAB 114 7 May 2025 The New South Wales Court of Appeal (Bell CJ, Leeming JA and Ball JA) makes supplementary orders CAB 114 17 June 2025 Special leave granted CAB 254 Dated 8 July 2025 Perry Herzfeld 02 8231 5057 pherzfeld@elevenwentworth.com Peter Tierney 02 9229 7333 mail@petertierney.net James McComish 03 9225 6827 jmccomish@vicbar.com.au

children.title: Mr McClung sexually abused by Fr Pickin
Mr McClung complained to Fr Doran, a priest within the Diocese, that he was sexually abused by Fr Pickin
Father Pickin attended Wallsend High School to provide religious education classes to students
The appellant, aged 13, is in year 8 (second form) at Wallsend High School, and is taught religious education by Fr Pickin

sentences.text:
H I G H C O U R T O F A U S T R A L I A
NOTICE OF FILING This document was filed electronically in the High Court of Australia on 08 Jul 2025 and has been accepted for filing under the High Court Rules 2004.
Details of filing and important additional information are provided below.

Details of Filing File Number: File Title: S94/2025 AA v. The Trustees of the Roman Catholic Church for the Diocese of Maitland-Newcastle ABN 79469343054 Registry: Sydney Document filed: Filing party: Date filed: Form 27B - Appellant's chronology Appellant 08 Jul 2025 Important Information This Notice has been inserted as the cover page of the document which has been accepted for filing electronically.
It is now taken to be part of that document for the purposes of the proceeding in the Court and contains important information for all parties to that proceeding.
It must be included in the document served on each of those parties and whenever the document is reproduced for use by the Court.

Date Event Reference 15 January 1969 Father Pickin appointed to Wallsend parish CAB 11; SC[10.5] 1969 1969 1969 1969 1969 1969 Mid 1969 Father Pickin attended Wallsend High School to provide religious education classes to students CAB 11; SC[10.5] The appellant, aged 13, is in year 8 (second form) at Wallsend High School, and is taught religious education by Fr Pickin CAB 11; SC[10.6], [10.7] A friend of the appellant, Mr Alan Perry, is also taught religious education by Fr Pickin CAB 38; SC[137] Fr Pickin invites both the appellant and Mr Perry to the Presbytery of St Patrick’s Church on multiple occasions CAB 11; SC[10.7] The appellant and Mr Perry attend the Presbytery of St Patrick’s Church with Fr Pickin on Friday nights on multiple occasions CAB 11; SC[10.7], [10.8] While at the Presbytery, Fr Pickin gave both the appellant and Mr Perry beer and cigarettes CAB 11; SC[10.9] The appellant was sexually abused on multiple occasions by Fr Ronald Pickin in the bedroom of the presbytery of St Patrick’s Catholic Church, Wallsend CAB 42; SC[154] CAB 48; SC[182] CAB 56; SC[213] Late 1970s BB sexually abused by Father Pickin CAB 28; SC[84] CAB 154; CA[100] 15 March 2024 Appellant commences proceedings in the Supreme Court of New South Wales 4 June 2024 Appellant files Amended Statement of Claim ABFM 10 12 June 2024 Appellant gives evidence on commission CAB 10; SC[7] 23-29 August 2024 Trial heard before Primary Judge (Schmidt AJ) 20 September 2024 Primary Judge (Schmidt AJ) delivers judgment on liability 18 October 2024 Primary Judge (Schmidt AJ) enters judgment for the applicant in the amount of $636,480.00 CAB 5 CAB 5 CAB 91

Date Event 4 November 2024 Respondent files a Notice of Appeal against the decision of the Primary Judge Reference CAB 105 26 November 2024 Appellant files Notice of Contention in the CAB 111 New South Wales Court of Appeal 27 February 2025 Hearing in the New South Wales Court of Appeal (Bell CJ, Leeming JA and Ball JA) CAB 114 15 April 2025 The New South Wales Court of Appeal (Bell CJ, Leeming JA and Ball JA) deliver reasons and orders, in respect of proceedings 2024/00406824 allowing the appeal CAB 114 7 May 2025 The New South Wales Court of Appeal (Bell CJ, Leeming JA and Ball JA) makes supplementary orders CAB 114 17 June 2025 Special leave granted CAB 254 Dated 8 July 2025 Perry Herzfeld 02 8231 5057 pherzfeld@elevenwentworth.com Peter Tierney 02 9229 7333 mail@petertierney.net James McComish 03 9225 6827 jmccomish@vicbar.com.au
